question.text: Refinancing a loan is advantageous when:

answer.text: A lower cost loan is available to repay the existing costlier loan
The borrower wants to increase the loan amount
The borrower has missed several EMI payments

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Refinancing makes sense when a lower interest rate loan is available. The savings on interest must be weighed against any processing fees or prepayment charges to ensure net benefit. **The Trap:** Refinancing when rates have increased would actually increase cost. It's beneficial only when cheaper alternatives are available. **Cross-Exam Context:** Loan refinancing is tested in X-A (Ch 4) and is a common advisory situation. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Refinancing is worthwhile when the rate differential is at least 0.5-1% and significant tenure remains — calculate the breakeven period after accounting for all charges.
